obligation_text: In order to facilitate the monitoring of the functioning of the high-risk AI system, the logging capabilities shall enable the recording of events relevant for: identifying situations that may result in the high-risk AI system presenting a risk; facilitating the post-market monitoring; and monitoring the operation of the high-risk AI system.
scoring_text: Skill in designing AI monitoring and logging systems that capture risk-relevant events, support post-market monitoring workflows, and enable operational monitoring of high-risk AI systems.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing AI monitoring and logging systems that capture risk-relevant events, support post-market monitoring workflows, and enable operational monitoring of high-risk AI systems.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 12(2) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.714, cosine: 0.646). Secondary: AG-S-006 (AG, cosine: 0.637), AG-T-009 (AG, cosine: 0.630).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.1/10 (raw 0.714). Shared vocabulary: [ai, risk, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [capture, designing, enable, events, high, logging] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.883 (P=0.883, R=0.884). Balanced P/R — the 24-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (85.4%). Semantic tension between "Skill in designing AI monitoring and logging systems that capture risk-relevant" and "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi". Despite STS-based 'Equal' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.646 < STS 0.714 (gap -0.069). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning between [capture, designing, enable] and [assessments, bias, conducting] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, risk, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [capture, designing, enable, events, high, logging]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 85.4% on Equal classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.714 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
